title: Dense Smoky Delight Thai Lettuce Nam-Prik
description: A smoky, pillowy, nostalgic Thai dish featuring iceberg lettuce, salt-bake, and low-glycemic-friendly ingredients.

ingredients.name: Iceberg Lettuce Leaves
ingredients.notes: Any other lettuce variety can be used as a substitute

ingredients.name: Smoked Salt
ingredients.notes: Kosher salt can be used as a substitute

ingredients.name: Chili Peppers
ingredients.notes: Adjust spiciness according to your preference

instructions: Rinse and dry the pandan leaves. Cut them into 4 equal pieces.
Place the coconut oil on the pandan leaves, then sprinkle smoked salt over the oil.
Place the pandan leaves with coconut oil and smoked salt on a baking sheet and bake for 5 minutes.
Remove the pandan leaves from the oven and let them cool.
In a mortar and pestle, pound the chili peppers until they are well-crushed.
Heat the chili peppers over low heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the palm sugar, lime juice, fish sauce, and rice vinegar to the mortar and pestle.
Stir the mixture well and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the salt-baked pandan leaves to the mortar and pestle and stir well.
Chop the iceberg lettuce leaves into small pieces and add them to the mortar and pestle.
Stir the mixture well and let it sit for 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
